title: STANDARD FOR DRIED OR DEHYDRATED CHILLI PEPPER AND PAPRIKA

sections.section: STANDARD FOR DRIED OR DEHYDRATED CHILLI PEPPER AND PAPRIKA
sections.summary: Document title only: Standard for dried or dehydrated chilli pepper and paprika.
Standard identifier CXS 353‑2022, adopted in 2022.

Applies to dried or dehydrated chilli pepper and paprika (as defined) offered for direct consumption, as a food ingredient or for repackaging, and explicitly excludes product intended for industrial processing.
Heading only — Description section.

Defines dried/dehydrated chilli pepper and paprika as products obtained by drying fruits of specified Capsicum species (listed in Table 1), with or without seeds or stalks, processed appropriately; Table 1 lists the common/trade and scientific names covered.

sections.section: 2.1.1 Dried or dehydrated chilli pepper and paprika is the product obtained from drying the fruits of Capsicum species of the family Solanaceae mentioned in Table 1, with or without seeds or stalks and processed in an appropriate manner.
sections.summary: Product definition: dried or dehydrated chilli pepper and paprika are obtained from drying fruits of the Capsicum species listed in Table 1, with or without seeds or stalks and processed appropriately.
Heading only — Description section (duplicate heading).

Specifies allowed styles — whole (with or without stalk), crushed/cracked/broken/flakes, and ground/powdered — defines ground chilli (may include placenta, seeds, calyx and stalk) and ground paprika (excludes placenta, seeds, calyx and stalk), notes color variations, and permits other distinct styles if properly labelled.

sections.section: Ground chilli pepper is the product obtained by grinding whole dried chilli with or without the placenta, seeds, calyx and stalk, and without any other added matter.
sections.summary: Ground chilli pepper is produced by grinding whole dried chilli with or without the placenta, seeds, calyx and stalk, with no other added matter, and may vary in colour depending on species/variety.

sections.section: Ground paprika is the product obtained by grinding whole dried paprika excluding the placenta, seeds, calyx and stalk, and without any other added matter.
sections.summary: Ground paprika is produced by grinding whole dried paprika excluding the placenta, seeds, calyx and stalk, with no other added matter, and may vary in colour from orange to red through various shades depending on species/variety.
Repeats the 2.2 Styles content: allowed styles, definitions for ground chilli and ground paprika, color variability, and allowance of other styles if labelled.

sections.section: 2.2.1 Ground paprika is the product obtained by grinding whole dried paprika excluding the placenta, seeds, calyx and stalk, and without any other added matter.
sections.summary: Defines ground paprika as the product obtained by grinding whole dried paprika with the placenta, seeds, calyx and stalk excluded and without any added matter, and states its colour may range from orange to pale reddish brown depending on species/varieties.
Section heading only: 'Essential composition and quality factors' — no substantive provisions in this item.
States that dried or dehydrated chilli pepper and/or paprika must conform to the descriptions provided in Section 2.

Sets quality factor requirements: the product must have characteristic organoleptic properties and be free from foreign odours/flavours/colours and adulteration; it must comply with chemical and physical requirements in Annex I (with allowed defects not affecting overall quality), classification into extra/class I/class II is optional (with class II as the minimum if unclassified), and sizing by length or trade practice is optional but the method must be declared when used.

Requires the product to have a characteristic odour, flavour and colour (which may vary by geo‑climatic conditions) and to be free from any foreign odour, flavour or colour—particularly rancidity and mustiness—and free from adulteration.

Requires dried or dehydrated chilli peppers and paprika to comply with the chemical and physical requirements set out in Annex I (Table 1 and Table 2), and states that allowed defects must not impair the product's general appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation.

States that classification of dried chilli peppers and paprika is optional and, per Annex I criteria, may be designated extra, class I (grade I) or class II (grade II); if traded unclassified, the physical and chemical requirements applicable to class II/grade II apply as the minimum.
Allows whole dried/dehydrated chilli peppers and paprika to be sized by length or by existing trade practices, and requires the sizing method to be indicated on the package when sizing is used.
Permits the use of anticaking agents listed in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in powdered forms of foods conforming to this standard.

Requires products to comply with maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995), the Code of Practice for Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017) and other relevant Codex texts, and to meet Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparing and handling products in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low‑Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015, Annex III on spices and dried culinary herbs) and other relevant Codex texts, and states products should meet any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.
Requires containers to be as full as practicable without impairing quality and to be consistent with a proper declaration of the product contents.
States that labelling must follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and that additional specific labelling provisions in this standard apply.
Specifies that the product's common name must follow Section 2.1.1, must include an indication of the style per Section 2.2, and that trade name, variety or cultivar may be shown on the label.

sections.section: 8.2.1 The common name of the product shall be as described in Section 2.1.1.
sections.summary: Requires the product's common name to be as described in Section 2.1.1.

sections.section: 8.2.2 The common name of the product shall include an indication of the style as described in Section 2.2.
sections.summary: Requires the product's common name to include an indication of the style as described in Section 2.2.

sections.section: 8.2.3 Trade name, variety or cultivar may be listed on the label.
sections.summary: Permits listing a trade name, variety or cultivar on the product label.

sections.section: 8.3 Country of origin and country of harvest
sections.summary: Requires declaration of country of origin and makes country of harvest, region of harvest and year of harvest optional.

sections.section: 8.3.1 Country of origin shall be declared.
sections.summary: Mandates that the country of origin be declared.
States that declaring the country of harvest is optional.

sections.section: 8.3.3 Region of harvest and year of harvest (optional).
sections.summary: Heading only: optional labelling element for region of harvest and year of harvest (no substantive provisions included).
Requires commercial identification to indicate class/grade where applicable and optionally the size for whole-style products.
States that labelling of non-retail containers must comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).
Section heading only for Methods of Analysis and Sampling (no substantive provisions in this item).
For compliance testing, specifies that the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234‑1999) relevant to this standard shall be used.
Sampling plan is not provided in the standard and is stated as 'to be developed' (no sampling procedures specified).

Annex I contains Table 2 setting maximum physical tolerances for dried/dehydrated chilli pepper and paprika by product, style and class/grade (limits for excreta, mould and insect damage, extraneous/foreign matter, live insects and specified counts for rodent hair/insect fragments, with some cells shown as N/A).

Bibliographic and reference notes listing Codex standards and codes cited (e.g., General Standards for Food Additives and Contaminants, Code of Practice for Mycotoxins in Spices, food hygiene and labelling standards, and Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling).
